mainEntity.name: What is Speakable schema, and do I really need it for voice search and answer engine optimization?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Speakable schema lets you flag specific sections of text as ideal for voice assistants to read aloud.

It doesn’t guarantee selection, but it helps systems understand which parts of your page are “answer ready.”

**Key factors**
- Mark up concise, self-contained answers rather than long paragraphs.
- Use it on pages that already have Q&A structures and FAQ schema.
- Keep tagged content under roughly 30–60 words per answer.
- Test implementations and watch for errors in structured data tools.

Start by adding Speakable markup to your most important FAQs or overview pages where you’d want a spoken response.

Consider it a helpful hint to voice assistants, layered on top of strong content and technical foundations.
acceptedAnswer.description: Speakable schema lets you tag specific text blocks as suitable to be read aloud by voice assistants. It’s not mandatory, but when combined with clear Q&A content and other schema, it can improve how cleanly your answers are extracted and increase the chances your site is used for spoken responses.
